text: He asks the first disciples what they seek and invites them to come and see where he dwells
He renames Simon son of Jonah as Cephas, meaning a stone, marking him with a new identity
He greets Nathanael as a true Israelite without guile and reveals he saw him under the fig tree before Philip called
He promises Nathanael will see greater things — heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man
He tells his mother at Cana that his hour has not yet come
He commands the servants to fill the waterpots with water and to draw out and bear it to the governor of the feast
He drives out the dove sellers from the temple, declaring that his Father's house must not be made a house of merchandise
He rebukes Nicodemus the master of Israel for not knowing these things, testifying of what he has seen and warning that those who reject earthly truths cannot grasp heavenly ones
He declares that condemnation comes because light entered the world but men loved darkness, while those who do truth come to the light so their deeds are shown to be wrought in God
He asks the Samaritan woman at the well to give him a drink
He tells the woman that if she knew the gift of God and who was speaking with her, she would have asked him for living water
He says whoever drinks of this well water will thirst again, contrasting it with the living water he gives
He tells the woman to call her husband and reveals he knows she has had five husbands and the man she now has is not her husband
He tells the woman the hour comes when worship will be neither at this mountain nor Jerusalem, and that salvation is of the Jews
He tells the disciples he has meat to eat that they know nothing of
His meat is to do the will of him who sent him and to finish his work — the fields are white for harvest, and reapers gather fruit unto life eternal so sower and reaper rejoice together
He rebukes the nobleman, saying that unless they see signs and wonders, they will not believe
He sends the nobleman home with the word that his son lives, healing the boy at a distance
He asks the lame man at the pool of Bethesda whether he wants to be made whole
He commands the lame man to rise, take up his bed, and walk
He finds the healed man in the temple and warns him to sin no more lest a worse thing come upon him
He defends his Sabbath healing by declaring that his Father works until now and he himself works
The Son can do nothing of himself but only what he sees the Father do, for the Father loves the Son and shows him all things, and will show greater works
He judges only as the Father directs and points to multiple witnesses — John the Baptist, his works given by the Father, and the Father himself — that they might be saved
He receives no honor from men but knows they lack the love of God; they reject him who comes in the Father's name yet would receive another, and Moses himself accuses them
He asks Philip where they shall buy bread to feed the great multitude that has come to him
He instructs the disciples to make the men sit down before the feeding of the five thousand
He tells the disciples to gather up the leftover fragments so that nothing be lost
He calls out to the terrified disciples on the sea, saying it is I — be not afraid
He rebukes the crowd for seeking him only because they ate the loaves and tells them to labor not for perishing meat but for the meat that endures unto everlasting life
He declares that the work of God is to believe on the one whom God has sent
Moses did not give the true bread from heaven; the Father gives it, and the bread of God comes down from heaven and gives life unto the world
He tells the crowd that even though they have seen him, they do not believe
He came down from heaven not to do his own will but the will of him who sent him, and the Father's will is that he lose none given to him but raise them up at the last day
He tells the crowd not to murmur among themselves
Everyone taught of God and who learns of the Father comes to him, no man has seen the Father except the one from God, and whoever believes on him has everlasting life
Their fathers ate manna in the wilderness and died, but this is the bread that comes down from heaven so that one may eat of it and not die
Unless one eats the flesh of the Son of Man and drinks his blood, he has no life in him
As the living Father sent him and he lives by the Father, so the one who eats him shall live by him; this bread from heaven gives life forever, unlike the manna
He asks the murmuring disciples whether his teaching offends them, and what if they should see the Son of Man ascend up where he was before
He declares that some among them do not believe, and that no man can come to him unless it is given by the Father
He asks the twelve whether they also will go away
He reminds the twelve that he has chosen them, yet one of them is a devil
He tells his brothers his time is not yet come while theirs is always ready, that the world hates him because he testifies its works are evil, and they should go up to the feast without him
His doctrine is not his own but his that sent him, and any man willing to do God's will shall know whether the doctrine is of God; he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true
He defends his Sabbath healing by appealing to the lawful practice of circumcision on the Sabbath, asking why they are angry that he made a man whole
He cries in the temple that he is not come of himself but is from him who sent him; he knows the Father, but they do not
He declares that yet a little while he is with them and then goes to him who sent him, and where he goes they cannot come
He cries on the great day of the feast that if any man thirsts, let him come to him and drink
He tells the accusers of the woman caught in adultery that he who is without sin among them should cast the first stone
He asks the woman where her accusers are and tells her that neither does he condemn her — go and sin no more
They judge after the flesh, but he judges no man; yet if he judges, his judgment is true because he is not alone but with the Father who sent him
He tells them they neither know him nor his Father; if they had known him, they would have known the Father also
He says he goes his way and they shall seek him and die in their sins, for where he goes they cannot come
Unless they believe that I am he, they shall die in their sins; he is the same he said from the beginning, and he speaks to the world what he has heard from the one who sent him
If they continue in his word, they are his disciples indeed
Whoever commits sin is the servant of sin, and the servant does not abide in the house forever, but the Son abides forever

Though they are Abraham's seed, they seek to kill him because his word has no place in them; they are of their father the devil — a murderer and liar from the beginning — and reject the truth he speaks from God
He has no devil but honors his Father; he seeks not his own glory, and if any man keeps his saying, he shall never see death
His honor comes not from himself but from the Father; though they claim God as theirs, they do not know him, but he knows the Father and keeps his saying
Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but the works of God should be made manifest in him; he must work the works of him that sent him while it is day, for night comes when no man can work
He sends the man born blind to wash in the pool of Siloam, and the man returns seeing
He finds the cast-out man and asks him whether he believes on the Son of God
He reveals himself to the healed man, declaring that the one who talks with him is the Son of God
He has come for judgment into this world, that those who do not see might see, and those who see might be made blind

He who does not enter the sheepfold by the door is a thief and robber, but the shepherd enters by the door, calls his own sheep by name, and they follow him because they know his voice — they will not follow a stranger
All who came before him are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them
The thief comes only to steal, kill, and destroy, but he has come that they might have life and have it more abundantly
The hireling, who is not the shepherd, sees the wolf coming and flees because he does not own the sheep, leaving them to be caught and scattered
As the Father knows him, so he knows the Father, and he lays down his life for the sheep; he has other sheep not of this fold whom he must bring, that there shall be one fold and one shepherd
They do not believe because they are not of his sheep, as he had said unto them
He asks for which of the many good works shown them from the Father do they seek to stone him
He cites the law that called them gods to whom the word of God came, asking why they accuse him of blasphemy for saying he is the Son of God whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world
